narrative: Czwarty dzień z rzędu — equity $96,636 (-3.88% / -$3,906 od startu). Portfel trzyma bearish PUT pozycje w risk_on rally — to mechanizm straty, nie błąd kodu. options_side_bias=long potwierdzony (rescue z 2026-05-09 już to ustawił; keepujemy). Dwa krytyczne problemy operacyjne wymagają działania dziś: (1) exit-emergency 4 placed/0 filled = stare LIMIT orders sprzed MARKET-patcha nadal wiszą jako open w Alpaca — manualne anulowanie pilne, dają fałszywe poczucie ochrony; (2) options-momentum 40% fill rate przez 2+ sesje z rzędu = tracimy 60% zamierzonej ekspozycji przez zbyt ciasne limity. UUID-keyed exits (7 entries) to SELL_TO_CLOSE bez client_order_id — blokują reconstruct_trades i trzymają cumulative_trades=0 po raz czwarty.
edge_assessment: Zero completed round-trips czwarty dzień z rzędu — brak danych per-strategy. Jedyny potwierdzony edge: geo-entries XOM/XLE/RTX 100% fill rate (3/4). Anti-edge aktywna: bearish PUTs w risk_on rally = płacimy za bycie po złej stronie rynku. exit-emergency-googl 1/1 potwierdza że MARKET patch działa dla nowych zleceń.

new_heuristic_proposals.title: Widen options entry limit by 8% above close_price to fix 40% fill rate
new_heuristic_proposals.rationale: options-momentum 40% fill rate (3/10 canceled) across 2+ sessions means 60% of intended options exposure never opens. limit_price=close_price is too tight relative to bid/ask spread at order placement time. Widening to close_price*1.08 would materially improve fills at minimal entry-price cost.
new_heuristic_proposals.implementation_sketch: options-monitor/monitor.py::find_and_execute_contract(): change limit_price = round(close_price, 2) to limit_price = round(close_price * float(os.getenv('OPTIONS_ENTRY_SLIPPAGE', '1.08')), 2). Add sanity cap: skip if limit_price > close_price * 1.15 (prevents runaway on wide-spread chains). Log new limit vs. close in [EXECUTED] email for audit. After deploy: monitor fill rate for 3 sessions — if still < 60%, escalate to using ask price from latest_quote endpoint directly.

new_heuristic_proposals.title: Cancel pre-patch exit-emergency LIMIT orders stuck open in Alpaca
new_heuristic_proposals.rationale: 4 exit-emergency orders (4 placed/0 filled/0 canceled) are stale LIMIT orders from before the MARKET-order patch. They provide phantom protection that won't execute in fast-market conditions. exit-emergency-googl 1/1 confirms new path works; old flat-ID orders need manual cleanup.
new_heuristic_proposals.implementation_sketch: Immediate manual: Alpaca dashboard -> Orders -> status=open -> cancel any orders where client_order_id == 'exit-emergency' (old flat format, no symbol suffix). Code (automated): add startup scan to exit-monitor — GET /v2/orders?status=open&limit=100, filter where client_order_id in ('exit-emergency',) (old flat format), DELETE /v2/orders/{id} each. Log count in notify_summary. Priority HIGH — stale emergency orders give false safety signal. 15 min manual + 45 min code for automated scan.

new_heuristic_proposals.title: Tag options-exit SELL_TO_CLOSE with client_order_id to fix 0-completed-trades in analyzer
new_heuristic_proposals.rationale: 7 UUID-keyed fill_rate entries are options-exit SELL_TO_CLOSE orders placed without client_order_id. Analyzer cannot pair them to entry orders, keeping cumulative_trades=0 and by_strategy empty for 4 days straight. Tagging exits with structured IDs would unblock round-trip reconstruction.
new_heuristic_proposals.implementation_sketch: options-exit-monitor/monitor.py: when building SELL_TO_CLOSE order payload add client_order_id=f'exit-tp-{sym.replace("/","-")}-{int(time.time())}' for TP exits and 'exit-sl-...' for SL exits. Verify analyzer.py::_is_close() detects 'exit-tp-*' and 'exit-sl-*' prefixes (per CLAUDE.md this was added — confirm in prod). In reconstruct_trades(): add fallback pairing by OCC contract symbol when full client_order_id chain is broken (entry='options-momentum-{contract}-{ts}', exit='exit-tp-{contract}-{ts}' — both contain contract OCC code, use as join key). This 2-file fix should resolve 0-completed-trades after next options exit cycle.
